title: Pilgrim's Progress §360: IGNOR.
body: IGNOR. What are good thoughts concerning God? CHR. Even as I have said concerning ourselves, when our thoughts of God do agree with what the Word saith of him; and that is, when we think of his being and attributes as the Word hath taught, of which I cannot now discourse at large; but to speak of him with reference to us: Then we have right thoughts of God, when we think that he knows us better than we know ourselves, and can see sin in us when and where we can see none in ourselves; when we think he knows our inmost thoughts, and that our heart, with all its depths, is always open unto his eyes; also, when we think that all our righteousness stinks in his nostrils, and that, therefore, he cannot abide to see us stand before him in any confidence, even in all our best performances.
author: engine

witnesses.label: First edition Nathaniel Ponder, London 1678 — original imprint
Cited extensively by Spurgeon, Edwards, modern Reformed writers
